question_text: Two hairdryers have the same power rating of 1500 W. One is used for 5 minutes and the other for 10 minutes. Calculate how much more energy the second hairdryer uses.
explanation: Energy = Power × Time. First hairdryer: 1500 W × 300 s = 450,000 J. Second: 1500 W × 600 s = 900,000 J. Difference = 450,000 J = 450 kJ. The second hairdryer uses 450 kJ more energy because it runs twice as long.
